description:
You are a backup engineer who has just finished copying data that lives in
/home/user/project_files.  
Verify the integrity of that data and create a
compressed, dated backup.  When you are finished, three new filesystem
items must exist inside /home/user/backups (create the directory if it does
not already exist):

1. /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231                • a **directory** that is an exact, recursive copy of /home/user/project_files  
2. /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231.tar.gz         • a **gzip-compressed TAR archive** of the directory shown in item 1  
3. /home/user/backups/integrity_20231231.sha256             • a **plain-text file** that contains the SHA-256 checksum of the archive shown
   in item 2, in the exact format produced by the command  
   `sha256sum project_files_20231231.tar.gz`

Required format of integrity_20231231.sha256
• Exactly ONE line.  
• The line must contain two whitespace-separated fields:  
  a) the 64-character lowercase hexadecimal checksum  
  b) two consecutive spaces followed by the literal string  
     “project_files_20231231.tar.gz” (exact spelling, including the “.gz”).  
  Example of the required layout (the checksum below is **only an example**):  
  ```
  e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  project_files_20231231.tar.gz
  ```

Success criteria (all must be met):

• The directory shown in item 1 exists and contains exactly the same files that
  appear in /home/user/project_files (no extra files, no missing files).  
• The archive shown in item 2 exists and, when extracted, recreates the
  directory structure identical to item 1.  
• The integrity file shown in item 3 exists, contains one and only one line,
  follows the format specified above, and the checksum recorded in that file
  matches the checksum of the archive in item 2 when independently verified
  with `sha256sum`.  

Perform whatever commands you deem necessary (no root privileges available)
to satisfy all of the above requirements.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent before the task starts
----------------------------------------------------------

1. Directory tree:
/home/user/project_files
└── file1.txt         (contents: “Hello Backup\n”)
/home/user/project_files/file2.txt
                     (contents: “Data Integrity Test\n”)

2. The directory /home/user/backups does **not** exist yet.

Verification points used by the automated test
----------------------------------------------

After the agent finishes, the tester will confirm that:

A. /home/user/backups exists and is a directory.

B. /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231 is a directory and contains
   exactly the two files:
   • /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231/file1.txt
   • /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231/file2.txt  
   Each file’s contents must match byte-for-byte with the respective file in
   /home/user/project_files.

C. /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231.tar.gz exists, is a valid gzip
   archive, and when extracted produces a directory tree identical to B.

D. /home/user/backups/integrity_20231231.sha256 exists, is a text file,
   contains a single line matching the regular expression:

   ^[0-9a-f]{64}  project_files_20231231\.tar\.gz$

   (note the TWO consecutive spaces between the checksum and the filename).

E. The SHA-256 checksum recorded inside integrity_20231231.sha256 is exactly
   the checksum produced by running

   sha256sum /home/user/backups/project_files_20231231.tar.gz

   inside the container.

No fixed checksum value is hard-coded in the tests; instead, the test script
computes it at runtime and compares it with the line found in the integrity
file.
